name: Parque Natural dels Ports – circular por Estrets d'Arnes y las Roques de Benet
description: This moderate 16.2-mile mountain biking route in Parque Natural dels Ports explores Estrets d'Arnes gorge and Roques de Benet.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Horta de Sant Joan are set within the Els Ports Natural Park, a region characterized by imposing mountains, olive groves, and vineyards. The landscape features distinctive rock formations like Roques de Benet and is traversed by rivers such as the Algars, Estrets, and Canaletes. A significant draw for cyclists is the Via Verde de la Terra Alta, an old railway line converted into a dedicated path. This varied terrain offers a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Horta De Sant Joan The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural dels Ports – circular por Estrets d'Arnes y las Roques de Benet, a 16.2 miles (26.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park, passing through the Estrets d'Arnes gorge and near the iconic Roques de Benet. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Old railway viaduct loop from Horta de Sant Joan, a difficult 33.6 miles (54.1 km) path. This route follows the historic railway line, offering scenic views as it winds through forests and mountains. Local mountain bikers also love the Horta de Sant Joan – Bridge Over the Algars River loop from Horta de Sant Joan, a 48.3 miles (77.8 km) trail leading through the rural landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Horta de Sant Joan is defined by the diverse terrain of Els Ports Natural Park, the accessible Via Verde de la Terra Alta, and the distinctive Roques de Benet. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Horta de Sant Joan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Horta de Sant Joan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Horta de Sant Joan. These include 4 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes, offering options for various skill levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38249422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Bot Station Bar (Railbus) loop from Horta de Sant Joan&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route suitable for families. The Via Verde de la Terra Alta is an old railway line converted into a paved path, providing accessible and picturesque routes for all ages.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: The terrain around Horta de Sant Joan is highly varied, ranging from winding roads through olive groves and vineyards to technical trails within the Els Ports Natural Park. You'll encounter everything from paved sections on the Via Verde to challenging climbs and thrilling descents with natural obstacles.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see while mountain biking in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: You can explore distinctive rock formations like the Roques de Benet, traverse the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Estrets Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or ride alongside the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5932096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canaleta River&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also rich with olive groves, almond trees, and vineyards, offering a purely rural and natural setting.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946120646/parque-natural-dels-ports-circular-por-estrets-d-arnes-y-las-roques-de-benet" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural dels Ports – circular por Estrets d'Arnes y las Roques de Benet&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop exploring the natural park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horta de Sant Joan – Bridge Over the Algars River loop from Horta de Sant Joan&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the rural landscape.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Horta de Sant Joan?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the accessible Via Verde de la Terra Alta, and the distinctive natural features like the Roques de Benet, which offer a wide range of experiences for different ability levels.

Q: Can I rent mountain bikes in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: Yes, local companies like Montsport offer bicycle rental services in Horta de Sant Joan, making it convenient to explore the trails even if you don't bring your own bike.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: Horta de Sant Joan is generally suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while summer can be warm but still enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons. The varied terrain means there are options even during different weather conditions.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails with cafe stops or amenities nearby?
A: Yes, routes along the Via Verde de la Terra Alta often pass through or near towns where you can find cafes and other amenities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38249422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Bot Station Bar (Railbus) loop from Horta de Sant Joan&lt;/a&gt; specifically mentions a stop at Bot Station Bar.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The Els Ports Natural Park offers extensive trails with varied and challenging terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19829345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Old railway viaduct loop from Horta de Sant Joan&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 33.6-mile path that follows the historic railway line, offering scenic views and a demanding ride through forests and mountains.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Horta de Sant Joan?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Horta de Sant Joan, especially near trailheads and access points for the Via Verde de la Terra Alta. Specific parking information for individual routes can often be found on komoot tour pages.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Els Ports Natural Park provides many opportunities for stunning views. Trails that ascend into the park, particularly those near the Roques de Benet or Santa Bàrbara Mountain, often feature incredible panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape, including olive groves, vineyards, and the dramatic rock formations.
tips: The Estrets river, with crystal clear waters, runs between cliffs and ravines in the middle of pine forests, scrub and meadows, Natural Park of Els Ports. With an irregular flow, the river jumps from raft to raft between rocks that form some curious walls.
El Toll Blau is the only pool in which bathing is allowed on the entire Camino de les Estrets.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Parque Natural dels Ports – circular por Estrets d'Arnes y las Roques de Benet mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this circular mountain bike route as moderate, suggesting it's suitable for various skill levels with good fitness. However, be prepared for some challenging, technical sections, especially around the iconic Estrets d'Arnes and Roques de Benet, where you might need to dismount and walk your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features highly varied terrain. You'll encounter accessible paths through forests and open valleys, particularly around Estrets d'Arnes. However, as you approach Roques de Benet, the terrain becomes significantly more rugged and rocky. Some sections may involve scrambling or carrying your bike over very technical ground.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several stunning highlights. You'll pass through the dramatic gorge of Estrets d'Arnes with its towering vertical walls and crystal-clear waters, including the beautiful Toll Blau. Further along, you'll encounter the impressive Roques de Benet, offering panoramic views from their summit. The Ferrassó Waterfall is another notable natural feature.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route, you can typically find parking in the nearby villages of Arnes or Horta de Sant Joan. These villages serve as common starting points for exploring the Els Ports Natural Park and offer amenities before and after your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural dels Ports – circular por Estrets d'Arnes y las Roques de Benet route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Dogs are generally allowed on trails within the Parque Natural dels Ports. However, to protect the local wildlife and livestock, it is essential to keep your dog on a leash at all times. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Els Ports Natural Park is beautiful year-round, but for mountain biking, spring and autumn are generally ideal. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and some sections might be challenging in wet winter conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: Typically, no special permits or entrance fees are required to access the trails within the Parque Natural dels Ports for recreational activities like mountain biking. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 26 km long with over 600 meters of elevation gain. Komoot estimates the duration to be around 2 hours and 47 minutes of riding time. However, due to the varied terrain and technical sections, especially around Roques de Benet, allow extra time for breaks, enjoying the views, and potentially walking unrideable sections.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in Els Ports Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Els Ports Natural Park is known for its wild beauty and diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for local fauna such as wild goats and various bird species, including vultures, which are often spotted soaring above the impressive rock formations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. You will find sections that are part of the Horta - Arnes - Els Ports and Pedals dels Ports routes, as well as smaller portions of Estels del Sud and Pista de les Eres.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the nearby villages of Arnes and Horta de Sant Joan, which are common access points for the park, offer various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in these charming villages to refuel and relax before or after your ride.
